title: Boracay 7-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Boracay 7-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Boracay, Philippines: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island) and Parasailing at White Beach. Prices from $1926 per person (estimate).
highlights: Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island)
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Boracay, Philippines — Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 30-32°C), with a wetter, occasionally typhoon-affected season June to October. November to April — the dry season, with calm seas and consistent sunshine. This itinerary blends Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island), Parasailing at White Beach, White Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island), Parasailing at White Beach, White Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Boracay
itinerary.description: Land at Godofredo P. Ramos Airport (Caticlan) (MPH) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island) — Full-day boat trip to snorkeling spots and sea caves around Boracay. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island)

itinerary.title: Boracay: Parasailing at White Beach & Helmet diving
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Parasailing at White Beach — Aerial views of the island from a towed parasail. In the afternoon, continue to Helmet diving — Walk on the seabed in a diving helmet without needing to swim. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Boracay: Mount Luho viewpoint zipline & White Beach
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mount Luho viewpoint zipline — Zipline ride to the island's highest viewpoint. In the afternoon, continue to White Beach — Boracay's famous 4km stretch of powder-white sand. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Crystal Cove Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Crystal Cove Island, a popular side trip from Boracay. Return to Boracay in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Crystal Cove Island

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Bulabog Beach kite-surfing side (quieter, different vibe from White Beach) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Bulabog Beach kite-surfing side (quieter, different vibe from White Beach)

itinerary.title: Boracay: D'Mall shopping and dining strip & Puka Shell Beach
itinerary.description: Morning visit to D'Mall shopping and dining strip — Open-air pedestrian mall at White Beach's center. In the afternoon, continue to Puka Shell Beach — Quieter, shell-strewn beach on the island's north end. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MPH for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book White Beach accommodation on the Station 1 or 2 stretch for the best sand and sunset views.
Island-hopping tours are cheaper and just as good booked through your resort or a beachfront kiosk rather than online in advance.
Visit Puka Shell Beach for a quieter alternative to the busier White Beach.
April-May can be extremely hot and crowded (LaBoracay season) — plan accordingly.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Waterproof phone pouch for boat trips
Sandals suited to sand and boat decks
thingsToDo: Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island)
thingsToAvoid: Bringing single-use plastic bags or bottles onto the beach — they're banned.
Swimming at Bulabog Beach during kitesurfing season without checking conditions.
Overpaying unlicensed tour touts — book through your resort or a marked booth.
topRestaurants: Sunny Side Cafe — International breakfast, beachfront ($$)
nightlife: Station 2 beach bars and fire dancers
hiddenGems: Bulabog Beach kite-surfing side (quieter, different vibe from White Beach)
luxuryExperiences: Beachfront villa at a five-star White Beach resort
Spa day with an ocean-view treatment room
budgetExperiences: D'Talipapa market meal (buy seafood, have it cooked nearby)
localEtiquette: Filipino hospitality is warm — a smile and "po/opo" politeness markers are appreciated even from visitors attempting basic phrases.
Beachwear is fine on White Beach but cover up when leaving the immediate beachfront.
Environmental fees and rules (island closures, single-use plastic bans) are enforced — respect posted signage.
localTransportGuide: Boracay is compact — walking, tricycles (motorized rickshaws), and e-trikes cover the island easily and cheaply. Getting to the island itself involves a boat from Caticlan or Kalibo jetty port after flying into the nearest airport.
bestTimeToVisit: November to April — the dry season, with calm seas and consistent sunshine.
weather: Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 30-32°C), with a wetter, occasionally typhoon-affected season June to October.
visaInformation: The Philippines is visa-free for stays up to 30 days for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India.
averageFlightTime: ~11.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Boracay?
faqs.answer: The Philippines is visa-free for stays up to 30 days for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Boracay?
faqs.answer: November to April — the dry season, with calm seas and consistent sunshine. Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 30-32°C), with a wetter, occasionally typhoon-affected season June to October.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Boracay safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Boracay is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Boracay?
faqs.answer: The local currency is PHP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Boracay is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private sunset sailboat (paraw) cruise experience.

reviews.author: Mohammed Müller
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island) alone was worth the trip, but combined with D'Mall shopping and dining strip this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Anna Smith
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Boracay is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Island-hopping boat tour (Crystal Cove, Crocodile Island). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen

reviews.author: Mia van Dijk
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Parasailing at White Beach and D'Mall shopping and dining strip. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Marco Kim
reviews.title: Worth every penny
